preview_text:
The model rates England as a strong favorite with a 78% win probability, leaving DR Congo just a 3% chance of victory. This reflects a substantial gap in the forecast—one team is heavily favored while the underdog faces a steep climb.

The draw carries meaningful weight at 19%, suggesting the match may not be settled decisively despite England's advantage. The spread between England's win probability and the combined alternatives is wide, yet the presence of a near-one-in-five draw chance means a competitive outcome remains plausible rather than a foregone rout.

The model and sportsbooks are closely aligned. The consensus gives England 75%, compared to the model's 78%—a difference of 3 percentage points. Draw probabilities are nearly identical across both sources. The main divergence appears in the tail: books assign DR Congo a 7% underdog win rate versus the model's 3%, a gap the dashboard does not adjudicate. Both frameworks treat England as the clear favorite.
